description: Defines when to use IT Product vs. IT Component
examples: Business Capability → IT Product; Technical Capability → IT Component

description: Logical bundle of business functions, may include custom or third-party software
Classification of common tools and systems
examples: Thunder = IT Product; Python = IT Component

description: When to model separate IT Products for the same software
Ensures correct modeling by business and IT roles
examples: Business units define context-specific IT Products

Category, Naming, Versioning, Description, Interfaces, Architecture, etc.
Defines the type of IT Product
Should reflect business use and be unique
Functional versioning, not technical
In English, include GoBD relevance if applicable
Link to Interface Fact Sheets
Defines business alignment
Includes hosting type, software, OS, DB, runtime, etc.

For strategic evaluation
The IT Product is influenced or impacted by strategic or operational initiatives.
The IT Product is part of or supported by a Digital Solution, especially in agile (SAFe) contexts.
Abusiness unit or team is accountable for the lifecycle and governance of the IT Product.
The IT Product either offers or uses interfaces to exchange data or functionality.
The IT Product enables or automates specific business processes.
The IT Product contributes to the realization of one or more business capabilities.
The IT Product depends on technical components (e.g., software, platforms) for implementation.
The IT Product is linked to architectural decisions that guide its design and evolution.
High-level strategic alignment of the IT Product with business goals.
Captures the business processes and capabilities supported by the IT Product.
Describes the technical components and platforms required by the IT Product.
